_schema.description: Two-level hierarchical dataset: global R&D spending (billions USD) by sector and subcategory. 6 parent sectors, 28 leaf subcategories. Values represent approximate annual spend. Designed to produce a visually interesting squarified treemap with one clearly dominant sector (Technology) and meaningful subcategory variation within each.
_schema.structure: Root node contains a 'children' array of sector objects. Each sector has a 'name' and a 'children' array of subcategory objects. Each subcategory has a 'name' and 'value' (spend in $B). Sectors do not have a 'value' field — D3 hierarchy.sum() computes parent totals from children.
_schema.replace: To use your own data: (1) replace 'name' at root level with your dataset title, (2) replace sector names and subcategory names with your categories, (3) replace 'value' fields with your actual quantities. Values must be positive numbers. You can have as many or few children per sector as your data requires. More than 8 sectors or 40 subcategories will produce very small leaf cells.
